items.question_text: Complete: Departure information contained in the ATIS broadcast may be omitted if the pilot states the appropriate ATIS _____.
items.explanation: When the pilot states they have the current ATIS code, departure information may be omitted — the pilot already has it.

choices.text: code

items.question_text: Under 3-9-2, when a departure delay is expected, the controller must advise the pilot of the delay as soon as _____.
items.explanation: Delay advisories must be issued 'as soon as practical' — the controller doesn't wait for a specific threshold.

choices.text: practical

items.question_text: Under 3-9-3, departure control instructions must include initial heading, altitude, and what other element when specified in a letter of agreement?
items.explanation: Departure control instructions must include frequency and beacon code when specified in a letter of agreement in addition to heading and altitude.

choices.text: Departure frequency and beacon code when specified in an LOA.
Taxi route to the active runway.
Expected delay time and sequence number.
Runway condition code and braking action advisory.

items.question_text: Complete: Do not authorize an aircraft to LUAW if it has been holding for more than the time specified in the facility directive without additional information being provided.
items.explanation: The LUAW (Line Up and Wait) procedure has a time limit specified in the facility directive. The controller must track how long an aircraft holds in position.

choices.text: LUAW

items.question_text: Under 3-9-5, a controller may issue a takeoff clearance with an aircraft still on the runway when separation will exist at the time the departure begins its takeoff roll. This is known as what?
items.explanation: Anticipating separation allows issuing a takeoff clearance to a subsequent aircraft while the preceding aircraft is still on the runway, based on the expectation that separation will exist when the subsequent aircraft starts its roll.

choices.text: Sequenced departure with rolling takeoff authorization.
Anticipating separation.
Reduced runway separation departure.
Conditional takeoff clearance.

items.question_text: Complete: Separate a departing aircraft from a preceding departing or arriving aircraft using the same runway by ensuring it does not begin takeoff roll until the other aircraft has _____ and is airborne.
items.explanation: The basic same-runway departure separation requires the preceding aircraft to have crossed the departure end or turned to avoid conflict before the next begins rolling.

choices.text: crossed the runway end or turned

items.question_text: Under 3-9-7, what additional separation consideration applies when an aircraft departs from a runway intersection?
items.explanation: Wake turbulence separation for intersection departures requires additional time or distance spacing when a smaller aircraft departs from an intersection behind a larger aircraft that departed from the full length.

choices.text: Intersection departures require the same separation as full-length departures.
Wake turbulence separation requires additional spacing between certain aircraft when one departs from an intersection behind a larger aircraft from the full length.
Intersection departures may always depart immediately regardless of preceding aircraft size.
Only visual separation may be used for intersection departures.

items.question_text: Under 3-9-8, operations on intersecting runways require separation from aircraft operating on the _____ runway.
items.explanation: The intersecting runway is the other runway that physically crosses the departure runway.

choices.text: intersecting

items.question_text: Under 3-9-9, nonintersecting converging runway operations require ensuring that aircraft do not pass within the area where flight paths _____.
items.explanation: Nonintersecting converging runways may have flight paths that cross. The controller must ensure aircraft do not pass through the intersection of those flight paths simultaneously.

choices.text: converge or intersect

items.question_text: Complete: Issue the takeoff clearance once the aircraft is in position and takeoff _____ has been applied.
items.explanation: Takeoff clearance requires the controller to verify separation before issuing the clearance.

choices.text: separation

items.question_text: Under 3-9-11, what must a controller do when canceling a takeoff clearance?
items.explanation: When canceling a takeoff clearance, the controller must transmit the aircraft identification, state 'CANCEL TAKEOFF CLEARANCE,' and provide the reason.

choices.text: Simply key the microphone rapidly — the pilot will understand the cancellation.
Transmit the aircraft identification and state the cancellation with the reason.
Wait for the aircraft to begin its roll and then use the light gun red signal.
Transfer communications to ground control who will issue revised taxi instructions.
